title: Pseudo Ctags scanner for Go/Rust/Java/Kotlin/Ruby with graceful fallback
purpose: Scans source files using universal-ctags to extract structural method information for supported languages, with fallback behavior when ctags is unavailable.
module_context: Exports CtagsAvailability and CtagsScanOptions interfaces, defines constants for supported extensions, languages, timeouts, and batch limits. Includes FUNCTION_KINDS set for filtering method types.

steps.content: Spawn a process to execute ctags --version command.
Set up timeout to kill the process if it takes longer than DETECT_TIMEOUT_MS.
Capture stdout and stderr from the ctags process.
IF the process fails to start, resolve with unavailable status and error message.
IF the process exits with non-zero code, resolve with unavailable status and error details.
ELSE parse the version string from stdout to determine if it's Universal Ctags and extract version number.
Resolve with available status, isUniversal flag, and version information.
Filter input file paths to only include those with supported extensions.
IF no files match the extensions, return an empty result map.
IF the abort signal is already triggered, return an empty result map.
Process files in batches of MAX_FILES_PER_BATCH to avoid exceeding system limits.
IF the abort signal is triggered during processing, stop and return current results.
Run ctags on each batch and merge results into the final map.
IF a batch fails, log warning and continue with remaining batches.
Spawn a ctags process with JSON output format and specified languages.
Set up timeout to kill the process if it takes longer than SCAN_TIMEOUT_MS.
Set up abort listener to terminate process if signal is triggered.
Accumulate stdout data and parse JSON lines as they arrive.
IF the process fails to start, reject with the error.
IF the process exits with non-zero code, reject with error message from stderr.
ELSE resolve with the parsed structural methods grouped by file path.
Parse the JSON line from ctags output.
IF parsing fails or the line is not a tag, return early.
IF the tag lacks name or path, return early.
IF the tag kind is not a function type, return early.
Convert the ctags JSON tag to a StructuralMethod object.
Add the method to the output map under its file path.
Extract start and end line numbers from the JSON tag.
Parse the raw signature string to extract parameter types.
Determine if the method is exported based on access or language-specific rules.
Build and return a StructuralMethod object with all parsed information.
Trim the signature string and remove outer parentheses.
Split the signature into segments at top-level commas.
Extract type tokens from each segment.
Join the extracted types with commas and return the normalized parameter string.
Initialize tracking variables for parsing nested structures.
Iterate through each character in the input string.
Track nesting depth for brackets and parentheses.
Track string literals to avoid splitting inside quoted content.
IF a delimiter is found at top level, extract and store the segment.
Return the list of parsed segments.
Find the colon separator in the segment to extract type information.
IF a colon exists, return the part after it.
ELSE split the segment by whitespace and return the last part.
IF access information is present, return true if it's public or default.
ELSE IF the language is Go, check if the first character is uppercase.
ELSE return true as default exported status.
IF the kind is undefined, return false.
ELSE check if the kind is in the set of function kinds.
